issues.title: Review what is in ChatOptions
issues.body:
There is the issue #1271 to review but also that the new `o1` model has `temperature, top_p,and n` fixed at `1` while `presence_penalty and frequency_panalty` are fixed at `0`.  Also, `logprobs` is no supported.

This [link](https://medium.com/@woyera/whats-up-with-openai-s-new-o1-preview-and-o1-mini-models-7d788b37355f) has a nice write-up on limitations in the model that distinguish it from other models, notably that system messages, tools, function calling are not supported.

Also `top_k` is in common options but not supported in openai - i think i made the wrong analysis way back by putting it in common options.

comments.body:
@markpollack @sobychacko 
In regards to Gemini models: they support "stop sequences", "frequence penalty" and "presence penalty" - [link](https://ai.google.dev/api/generate-content)

We decided not to remove any parameters from `DefaultChatOptions` and provide a warning when applications try to use unsupported parameters in the respective models. Please take a look below for more details.

Anthropic Claude: Warning logs for frequency or presence penalty options, which the Anthropic API ignores

Bedrock: Added warnings when frequency penalty, presence penalty, or topK options are used, as Bedrock does not support these.

OpenAI: Added warning when topK parameter is passed, as this is not supported for OpenAI chat models.

In addition, frequency and presence penalty options were added to the `VertexAiGeminiChatOptions`. 

Here is the PR for reference: https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/pull/3033
